ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn input supports type=file with field composition, while the consumer owns trigger treatment and bounded acceptance guidance.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora keeps one real file input over the complete visible action so focus, picker security, form submission, disabled omission, and reset remain browser-owned, with optional normalized acceptance guidance.

visualSignature.summary: The trigger uses the shared ink action boundary, restrained control radius, strong label weight, preferred touch target, and violet focus treatment with forced-color mappings.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A bounded list of MIME or extension tokens is normalized, checked for duplicates, sorted, and applied to the native picker without replacing server-side validation.

api.enableWhen: Supply one through 32 valid MIME or extension tokens in acceptedFileTypes.

disabledBehavior.ui: No component-generated type guidance is shown and the native input has no accept attribute.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The browser picker remains unrestricted by file type and the component performs no type filtering.
disabledBehavior.events: onSelect receives the native FileList snapshot without any type-specific event or rejection branch.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No type-related accessible description, state, or hidden announcement is emitted.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native naming, keyboard picker activation, form/reset behavior, touch targets, axe, RTL, forced colors, and reduced motion are automated; required manual assistive-technology and device picker records remain absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: The focused browser suite verifies native keyboard activation, file chooser results, form serialization and reset, disabled omission, coarse-pointer activation, RTL, narrow layout, forced colors, and reduced motion.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual screen-reader, speech, switch, and mobile picker evidence required by the source contract is not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn supplies a file input rather than a drop, paste, and picker classifier with explicit rejection recovery.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora routes picker, paste, and asynchronous drop materialization through one bounded latest-request classifier with explicit accepted and rejected results; type and size preflight remain independently optional.

visualSignature.summary: A literal canvas surface, precise ink boundary, strong label hierarchy, and shared focus geometry become a solid active boundary during drop targeting without decorative motion.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional normalized type guidance is shared by the native picker and the paste/drop classifier so every intake path reports the same file-type rejection.

api.enableWhen: Supply acceptedFileTypes to add native picker guidance and classifier type checks.

disabledBehavior.ui: The picker has no accept attribute and the component adds no type-specific guidance.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Files of every type remain eligible for count and optional size classification.
disabledBehavior.events: onFiles never receives a file-type rejection created by the disabled branch.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The status region cannot announce a file-type rejection from this component.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional bounded client-side size preflight rejects oversized files consistently before consumer-owned upload transport begins.

api.enableWhen: Keep validateFileSize true and optionally set maxSizeBytes.

disabledBehavior.ui: The component renders no size-specific rejection or generated size guidance.
disabledBehavior.behavior: maxSizeBytes is ignored and oversized files continue through the remaining classifier branches.
disabledBehavior.events: onFiles never receives a file-too-large rejection created by the disabled branch.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The live status cannot announce a size rejection from this component.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Label and description wiring, polite status, picker/paste/drop recovery, axe, narrow RTL, forced colors, reduced motion, and touch are automated; manual assistive-technology and real-device drop/paste records remain absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: The focused browser suite verifies identical picker, paste, and drop classification, asynchronous latest-request ownership, adverse item recovery, bounds, disabled state, touch targets, RTL, and preferences.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual screen-reader, speech, switch, paste, drop, and real-device evidence required by the source contract is not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: The ordinary Shadcn progress component renders a progress indicator, while upload lifecycle, byte context, and announcement cadence remain consumer composition work.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora models consumer-owned upload lifecycle states with optional locale-aware byte context and bucketed announcements that avoid flooding assistive technology.

visualSignature.summary: A stable label, progress track, strong lifecycle state line, and restrained green success or ink-danger signal form a compact workbench status rail without layout-jumping decoration.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Paired uploaded and total byte counts add deterministic locale-aware IEC context to visible and programmatic progress values.

api.enableWhen: Supply valid uploadedBytes and totalBytes together.

disabledBehavior.ui: No byte totals are rendered in the progress value text.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Progress formatting uses percentage or lifecycle status only.
disabledBehavior.events: No byte-specific event, callback, or validation path is added.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: aria-valuetext and optional announcements contain no byte totals.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A polite atomic live region announces lifecycle changes and bounded percentage buckets instead of every rapid progress update.

api.enableWhen: Keep announceProgress true and optionally set announcementStep from one through 100.

disabledBehavior.ui: The visually hidden announcement node is not rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Progress and lifecycle rendering continue without announcement-state updates affecting output.
disabledBehavior.events: No announcement-specific event or callback is emitted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No aria-live or aria-atomic output is present.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Labelled determinate and indeterminate progress, value text, announcement throttling, lifecycle changes, axe, RTL, forced colors, and reduced motion are automated; manual screen-reader timing evidence remains absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: The focused browser suite verifies determinate and indeterminate lifecycle transitions, locale-aware bytes, bounded announcement buckets, disabled announcement output, narrow RTL, forced colors, and reduced motion.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual screen-reader announcement timing and speech output evidence required by the source contract is not recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn supplies a native file input and progress primitive, while queue validation, duplicate policy, ordered lifecycle, retry, cancellation, and recovery remain application composition.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora composes one bounded picker/paste/drop classifier with a controlled-or-uncontrolled ordered queue, optional duplicate/rejection recovery, and consumer-owned progress, retry, cancel, and removal lifecycle.

visualSignature.summary: The queue uses a literal canvas, strong ink rules, compact file hierarchy, restrained status signals, and the shared violet focus seam instead of nested cards or decorative upload chrome.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Type, size, duplicate, and visible rejection recovery are separately enabled around the shared intake classifier.

api.enableWhen: Enable only the preflight and recovery branches the product needs.

disabledBehavior.ui: No generated type/size/duplicate recovery list or live region is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Disabled classifiers and duplicate-key resolution are skipped; remaining count bounds still protect the queue.
disabledBehavior.events: No rejection produced solely by a disabled classifier and no recovery-specific event is emitted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No disabled recovery heading, list, aria-live region, or type/size/duplicate announcement exists.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Consumer status renders optional progress and enables separately gated retry, cancel, remove, and preview surfaces without starting transport.

api.enableWhen: Enable each lifecycle surface independently and supply only its consumer callback or renderer.

disabledBehavior.ui: No preview, progress, retry, cancel, or remove control is rendered for its disabled branch.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Status data remains inert queue data and disabled renderers are never invoked.
disabledBehavior.events: Disabled retry, cancel, and remove callbacks cannot fire.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No disabled progressbar, announcement, or action name appears in the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional Earlier and Later buttons expose the same immutable reorder protocol to keyboard, touch, speech, and switch input without requiring drag.

disabledBehavior.ui: Earlier and Later controls are absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Queue order changes only through controlled items or later selection.
disabledBehavior.events: No reorder change detail is emitted by the component.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No reorder action names or disabled boundary controls appear.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Labelled ordered queue, file-specific actions, keyboard reorder, native intake, axe, narrow RTL, forced colors, reduced motion, and reset are automated; risk-class manual assistive-technology and real-device queue records remain absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: Focused tests cover controlled/uncontrolled selection, type/size/duplicate rejection, recovery, ordered actions, consumer progress/retry/cancel/remove, form reset, touch targets, narrow RTL, and preferences.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Adapter fixtures and risk-class manual assistive-technology evidence remain unrecorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn supplies file input, avatar, button, and progress primitives, while preview memory, crop hooks, native form synchronization, replacement/removal, and server lifecycle remain application composition.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora keeps one native image input synchronized with controlled or default File state while optionally owning a revocable preview URL, metadata, crop/edit hook, recovery, and consumer server lifecycle.

visualSignature.summary: A precise bounded image well, literal canvas intake, strong label hierarchy, ink rules, compact metadata rail, and shared focus seam make avatar replacement recognizable without decorative framing.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional preview and file metadata use one abortable consumer transform and a component-owned object URL that is revoked after replacement, disablement, or unmount.

api.enableWhen: Enable preview and metadata independently; previewTransform runs only with showPreview.

disabledBehavior.ui: No image, preview loading/error status, or metadata description list is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No transform, object URL creation, revocation bookkeeping, or metadata formatting runs.
disabledBehavior.events: No preview-specific callback or event is emitted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No preview img, alternative text, preview status, or metadata terms appear.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Edit/crop, native removal, and persistent rejection recovery are independently enabled with ordinary named controls and no bundled editor.

api.enableWhen: Enable only the edit, remove, and recovery surfaces the product owns.

disabledBehavior.ui: Disabled edit, remove, and recovery controls or status are absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The native picker and selected File remain usable without crop or custom removal UI.
disabledBehavior.events: Disabled edit and remove actions cannot invoke consumer callbacks.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No disabled action names or rejection live region appears.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Optional UploadProgress and retry render only consumer-supplied server lifecycle; core performs no upload, storage, replacement, or deletion.

api.enableWhen: Enable lifecycle progress and retry independently around consumer status and callback data.

disabledBehavior.ui: No progressbar, lifecycle message, or retry button is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Upload status values remain inert and no network behavior exists.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No progress semantics, announcement, or retry action appears.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native picker/form semantics, preview alternative text, metadata terms, named actions, progress, recovery, axe, narrow RTL, forced colors, reduced motion, and reset are automated; risk-class manual assistive-technology and real-device picker records remain absent.

interactionEvidence.summary: Focused tests cover initial default and external controlled FormData, native selection, replacement, removal, reset, type/size recovery, preview URL lifecycle, crop hook, progress/retry, touch, narrow RTL, and preferences.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Crop/server adapter fixtures and risk-class manual assistive-technology evidence remain unrecorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.
